hamlets.name: Sagaponack
hamlets.top_restaurant: Wölffer Kitchen — farm-to-table at the vineyard, the best room in the hamlet
hamlets.local_spot: The Seafood Shop — the best fish market on the East End, open year-round
hamlets.secret: The back road along Sagg Main — farmland on one side, ocean light on the other. Nothing like it on the East End.
hamlets.practical_note: No village center. You are here for the land and the light. Parking is never a problem.

hamlets.name: East Hampton Village
hamlets.anchor: Guild Hall — the cultural institution that has anchored the village since 1931
hamlets.top_restaurant: The Palm East Hampton — the institutional dining room of the East End since 1950
hamlets.local_spot: The Palm at Huntting Inn — the room where deals get made
hamlets.secret: Main Beach at 6am before the season starts. The most beautiful half-mile of sand on the Eastern Seaboard.
hamlets.practical_note: Village parking is tight in-season. The Herrick Park lot is the local move. Off-season, the village is yours.

hamlets.name: Bridgehampton
hamlets.anchor: Bridgehampton Commons — the working heart of the East End
hamlets.top_restaurant: Almond — year-round, honest, and the best room in the hamlet
hamlets.local_spot: Almond Restaurant — year-round, honest, and the best room in the hamlet
hamlets.secret: The Bridgehampton-Sag Harbor Turnpike at dusk. Farm stands, vineyards, and open sky in one straight line.
hamlets.practical_note: The most central hamlet on the East End. Equidistant to Southampton and East Hampton. Underrated as a base.

hamlets.name: Southampton Village
hamlets.anchor: The Parrish Art Museum — the institutional anchor of the western corridor
hamlets.top_restaurant: Sant Ambroeus Southampton — the room where old money eats lunch
hamlets.local_spot: Sant Ambroeus Southampton — the room where old money eats lunch
hamlets.secret: Jobs Lane off-season. The architecture, the hedgerows, and the social order all visible at once. No crowds.
hamlets.practical_note: The most governed hamlet on the East End. Architecture review board is strict. That is why the values hold.

hamlets.name: Water Mill
hamlets.anchor: Water Mill Museum — the original grist mill, still standing
hamlets.top_restaurant: Suki Zuki — the year-round Japanese anchor of the Water Mill corridor
hamlets.local_spot: Tate's Bake Shop — the local institution that became a national brand
hamlets.secret: The boardwalk across the marsh at sunrise. The most undervalued acreage on the East End sits right behind it.
hamlets.practical_note: No village center, no foot traffic. You are buying land and light. The buyer who sees land before address wins here.

hamlets.name: Amagansett
hamlets.anchor: The Stephen Talkhouse — the most legendary music venue on the East End
hamlets.top_restaurant: il Buco al Mare — the best Italian on the East End, full stop
hamlets.local_spot: Estia's Little Kitchen — now in Sag Harbor. The best breakfast on the East End, worth the drive.
hamlets.secret: Indian Wells Beach on a Tuesday in September. The hamlet that still feels like what the entire East End used to be.
hamlets.practical_note: The last hamlet before Montauk. Off-season advantage is significant. Year-round community is tight and real.

hamlets.name: Sag Harbor
hamlets.anchor: The American Hotel — the room that has hosted every important conversation in this village for 150 years
hamlets.top_restaurant: Le Bilboquet Sag Harbor — the room that defines the village's social season
hamlets.local_spot: Sen — the local Japanese restaurant that the whole village treats as its dining room
hamlets.secret: The windmill at the end of Main Street at golden hour. The whole village still runs on foot traffic and conversation.
hamlets.practical_note: The most walkable hamlet on the East End. No car needed on weekends. The ferry to North Haven is a two-minute drive.

hamlets.name: Springs
hamlets.anchor: Pollock-Krasner House — Jackson Pollock painted here because the light was honest and the rent was low
hamlets.top_restaurant: Bostwick's Chowder House — the year-round local institution on Three Mile Harbor
hamlets.local_spot: Bostwick's Chowder House — the year-round local institution on Three Mile Harbor
hamlets.secret: Accabonac Harbor at dawn. The dock meets the marsh and the land remembers what it was.
hamlets.practical_note: The most affordable entry point in the township. The buyer who understands trajectory buys Springs before the market does.

hamlets.name: Montauk
hamlets.anchor: Montauk Lighthouse — the end of the road and the beginning of the Atlantic
hamlets.top_restaurant: Harvest on Fort Pond — the anchor dining room at the end of the road
hamlets.local_spot: Gosman's Dock — the institution where the fishing boats still come in. Under new ownership as of 2024.
hamlets.secret: Camp Hero State Park at the very tip. Radar towers, bluffs, and the most dramatic coastline on the East End. No crowds, ever.
hamlets.practical_note: Raw, unhurried, and unapologetically its own. The East End's last wild place. Off-season advantage is the highest of any hamlet.

hamlets.name: Wainscott
hamlets.anchor: Georgica Pond — the institutional water Wainscott shares with East Hampton Village
hamlets.top_restaurant: Bridgehampton Inn Restaurant — the year-round anchor of the Wainscott corridor
hamlets.local_spot: Bridgehampton Inn Restaurant — the year-round anchor of the Wainscott corridor
hamlets.secret: The dune path to the ocean at the end of Beach Lane. Nothing between you and the horizon.
hamlets.practical_note: The smallest hamlet by volume. Two percent of East End transactions. The buyer who finds it before the market does wins.
